meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control (Advanced Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no version control. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits: Solve Version Control for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop losing code. Learn how a SaaS starter kit solves version control issues for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster than ever.
content.answer_block: The lack of version control is a ticking time bomb for any freelancer. By adopting a robust SaaS starter kit, you unify your codebase architecture, automate repository initialization, and enforce Git best practices. This transition ensures your client projects are audit-ready, scalable, and protected against catastrophic data loss.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a screen at 3 AM. The client wants a feature change, but your current folder structure is a graveyard of 'final_v2_real_final' directories. You’ve spent more time hunting for the correct file version than actually shipping the feature. This isn't just inefficient; it's a structural failure that compromises your professional reputation.

But what if your development environment was pre-hardened? A SaaS starter kit isn't just a boilerplate; it's a version-controlled foundation that turns chaos into a systematic assembly line. By integrating Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for intelligent CLI orchestration, you move from manual file management to automated, high-velocity shipping.

steps.title: Architectural Scaffolding
steps.description: Initialize your project with a production-grade directory structure that naturally supports Git branching and modular feature development.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular SaaS boilerplate architecture including /apps, /packages, and /infrastructure folders. Ensure all configurations support strict TypeScript and ESLint, and output a .gitignore file that excludes all non-versioned build artifacts.

steps.title: Version-Controlled State Management
steps.description: Establish a single source of truth for your state models to prevent drift between local development and production environments.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a centralized state management schema using Zod for validation. Ensure all state updates are logged via middleware to facilitate easier debugging and commit history tracking.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npx prisma migrate dev --name init_schema'

steps.title: Automated Semantic Commits
steps.description: Configure your environment to enforce conventional commit messages, making your history readable and audit-friendly for clients.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Setup a Husky and commitlint configuration that enforces conventional commits (feat, fix, docs, refactor). Ensure this is integrated into the starter kit startup sequence.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npx husky install && npx husky add .husky/commit-msg "npx --no -- commitlint --edit ${1}"'

steps.title: CI/CD Pipeline Integration
steps.description: Automate deployment triggers so that every merge to main results in a validated, version-stamped build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow for the starter kit that runs linting, tests, and a build check on every pull request. Add an automated semantic versioning step using standard-version.

steps.title: Environment Variable Lifecycle
steps.description: Manage sensitive configuration securely without committing secrets to your version control system.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a dotenv-vault configuration within the starter kit to ensure environment variables are encrypted and synced across developer machines without exposing plain-text keys.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npx dotenv-vault new && npx dotenv-vault login'

pro_tips: Use Git hooks to automatically run your test suite before any commit; if the tests fail, the commit is blocked, ensuring your history remains 'green'.
Adopt a 'feature-branch-per-ticket' policy strictly, even for solo projects, to keep your main branch deployable at all times.
Leverage Claude Code to automatically generate changelogs from your commit history, saving you hours of manual documentation for clients.
Always store your 'Infrastructure as Code' (Terraform/Pulumi) in the same repository as your app code to keep environment versions in sync.
Use a .env.example file with dummy values to ensure your team or future-you knows exactly which keys are required without leaking secrets.
resources: Git Best Practices for SaaS: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
content.next_steps: Begin by auditing your current project folder for 'orphaned' files, then initiate a fresh repo using the steps above to transition your workflow to a professional standard.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your file system and start building products that actually scale. With the right starter kit, you reclaim your focus and deliver professional-grade code every single time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
